english: dgu, po. (dgu, po.) the wild animals of the field Sch. — 5. snyiń _opyo (snying 'phyo) Sch., 'the heart is swelling, courage is rising'; however ṡeś-pa _opyo (shes pa 'phyo) Med., seems rather to imply: consciousness gives way, is wavering, flitting; sems _opyo (sems 'phyo) Lt.?
Lt. perh. = _opyáń-ba ('phyang ba); occasionally, like _opyońs-pa ('phyongs pa) used incorr. for mčóńs-pá (mchongs pa).
harlot, prostitute, byéd-pa (byed pa), to whore, to fornicate Lex.
v. _opyár-ba ('phyar ba), also for čór-ba ('chor ba); _opyór-po ('phyor po) for mčór-po (mchor po), hence _opyór-dga ('phyor dga') Sch. dandy, fop.

I. vb., also prá-ba (phra ba), pf. _opras ('phras), to kick, to jerk, to strike with the foot, _opra-ṡúgs ('phra shugs) a stroke or kick with the foot, byéd-pa (byed pa) to kick about with the feet, in a paroxysm of pain or anguish, Pth.; *ṭa-ṡag gyáb-pa* (rta shag rgyab pa), to give one a kick. — II. = prá-ba (phra ba), prá-mo (phra mo).

lam-_opráń (lam 'phrang), a footpath along a narrow ledge on the side of a precipitous wall of rock (not 'a defile or narrow pass' Sch.), frq.; bar-doi _opráń (bar do'i 'phrang) the road of the abyss of the bar-do (bar do), (as with us: the valley of death) frq. Thgr.; bár-doi _opráń-sgról (bar do'i 'phrang sgrol), prob. a prayer for deliverance from that abyss Thgr.

pf. and fut. _oprad ('phrad) to meet together; dań (dang) to meet with, to fall in with, to find; de dań _oprád-do (de dang 'phrad do), you shall see him Dzl.; de ni ńa dań _oprad mi tub (de ni nga dang 'phrad mi thub), him I cannot admit Dzl.; bdag dań _oprád-par ṡog čig (bdag dang 'phrad par shog cig), come to see me Dzl.; sńar ńa dań _oprád-pai _óg-tu (sngar nga dang 'phrad pa'i 'og tu) not until they have met me (sensu obscoeno) Dzl.; byis-pai ro žig dań _oprád-do (byis pa'i ro zhig dang 'phrad do) he found the dead body of an infant Dzl.; _oprad-tsams ('phrad mtshams) Sch., intersecting line of two plains, corner, angle.
= _oprá-ba ('phra ba) and _oKráb-pa ('khrab pa); _opráb-byéd-pa ('phrab byed pa) to flutter, of a bird wounded by a shot.

prob. to be regarded as a sbst., like druń (drung), mdun (mdun), sńa (snga) etc., expressing immediate nearness; 1. in reference to space, but seldom, as for instance _oprál-du kyi krid-de ('phral du khyi khrid de), having a dog near at hand Glr.; gen. 2. with respect to time: _oprál dań pugs ('phral dang phugs), what is going to happen immediately and at a later period, presence and futurity; _oprál-púgs-kyi gal-rkyén tams-čád sél-bar byed ('phral phugs kyi gal rkyen thams cad sel bar byed) Glr. to avert immediate and subsequent disasters; _oprál púgs gáń-la bzań ('phral phugs gang la bzang) that is good both for the nearest and the more distant future; _oprál dań yún-du ('phral dang yun du) now and for a long time to come; _oprál-sog-jog-méd-par ('phral gsog 'jog med par) without having gathered or laid up any thing for daily use Mil.; _oprál-gyi dug-tsugs ńán-pa ('phral gyi dug tsugs ngan pa) a poor temporary dwelling, or also: a common, ordinary dwelling, v. no. 3; _oprál-du ṡa yžan ma rnyed-de ('phral du sha gzhan ma rnyed de) as at the moment he was not able to procure any other meat Dzl.; _oprál-du sleb yoń ('phral du sleb yong) Mil. I shall come immediately; _oprál-du dgós-pai yo-byád ('phral du dgos pa'i yo byad) the things necessary for daily use Dzl.; _oprál-du _obyór-ba ma yin ('phral du 'byor ba ma yin) that is not to be had at a moment's bidding Dzl.; also postp. e.g.: deï _oprál-la pan (de'i 'phral la phan) that will help the moment directly after it; more frq. after verbal roots = ma-tág-tu (ma thag tu): pebs-_oprál (phebs 'phral) as soon as he had arrived Mil.; smras-_oprál (smras 'phral) as soon as it has been spoken S.g.; skyes-_oprál (skyes 'phral) immediately after birth Lt.; in compounds: _oprál-rkyéń ('phral rkyen), _oprál-dgós ('phral dgos), _oprál-púgs ('phral phugs) cf. above; _oprál-grig ('phral grig) finished, ready, prepared, in proper case, (vulgo, esp. in W., a word much used) *ṭal - ḍig čó - čé* ('phral sgrig bco bce) to prepare, to get ready. — 3. fig., common, ordinary, of daily occurrence, common-place, _oprál-skad ('phral skad) B., C., (W. *pál-kad* (phal skad)) common dialect; žes _oprál-skad-la snań (zhes 'phral skad la snang) so you may hear it in the language of the common people, Gram., Wdn.
pf. prál (phral), fut. dbral (dbral), imp. pról (phrol), vb.a. to _obrál-ba ('bral ba), to separate, to part, *ka ṭál-wa* (kha bral ba), id., C.; dań (dang) from;
